lede: A look at January 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Joachim Gauck and Gong Lijiao, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States Department of Homeland Security
events.desc: The United States Department of Homeland Security officially begins operation.

events.title: Hiten (spacecraft)
events.desc: Japan launches Hiten, the country's first lunar probe, the first robotic lunar probe since the Soviet Union's Luna 24 in 1976, and the first lunar probe launched by a country other than Soviet Union or the United States.

events.title: 1987 Forsyth County protests
events.desc: About 20,000 protestors march in a civil rights demonstration in Forsyth County, Georgia, United States.

events.title: Shoichi Yokoi
events.desc: Japanese Sgt. Shoichi Yokoi is found hiding in a Guam jungle, where he had been since the end of World War II.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: The 1st Australian Task Force launches Operation Coburg against the North Vietnamese Army and Viet Cong during wider fighting around Long Bình and Biên Hòa.

events.title: Franklin D. Roosevelt
events.desc: World War II: Franklin D. Roosevelt and Winston Churchill conclude a conference in Casablanca.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Allies bombard Bangkok, leading Thailand, then under Japanese control, to declare war against the United States and United Kingdom.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: British Grand Fleet battle cruisers under Vice-Admiral Sir David Beatty engage Rear-Admiral Franz von Hipper's battle cruisers in the Battle of Dogger Bank.

events.title: University of Calcutta
events.desc: The University of Calcutta is formally founded as the first fully fledged university in South Asia.

events.title: AD 41
events.desc: Claudius is proclaimed Roman emperor by the Praetorian Guard after they assassinate the previous emperor, his nephew Caligula.

births.name: Joachim Gauck
births.line: German pastor and politician, 11th President of Germany

births.name: Moon Jae-in
births.line: South Korean politician, 19th President of South Korea

deaths.text: Winston Churchill, English statesman, soldier, and writer, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Pratulin Martyrs (Greek Catholic Church)
Day of the Unification of the Romanian Principalities (Romania)
